From Jason Cox • Founder, Rebel Dividends
107 Weeks. Not One Missed.
Here Is Where We Stand and Where We Are Going.
RD closed today at $0.00171, down 4.7% on the week. We issued weekly dividend #107 — every one tax-free under IRC §301(c)(2). Reinvestors are now sitting at roughly +163% since the April 2024 pivot (a $100K stake is worth about $263K). Collectors are at roughly +84% on the same starting capital — and they have been pulling cash every single week of that run.
The macro setup that gets us to the next leg is the cleanest it has been since the pivot. AI stock concentration just hit dot-com levels. Gold and M2 are at all-time highs, but BTC is sitting below its September ATH. Silver did the same thing in 2023–2025 and then ran +111% in eight weeks. The bridge — Hyperliquid — has the highest fee capture of any DEX in crypto and a buyback engine that turns volume directly into HYPE price.
Below: the chart of where we have been, the data on what is about to break, and the two price-target paths from here.

The price pulled back this week. The structure did not. Higher highs, higher lows, 107 dividends paid through every dip along the way. This is what compounding looks like when the engine doesn't stop.
Section 2 • Warning Signs Flashing
AI Concentration Hit 40% of the S&P. Calls Hit 60% of Options. Every Bubble Top Looks Like This.
The AI Big 10 Are Now 40% of the S&P 500. That Number Marked Every Bubble Top in History.
Bank of America's chart of top-10 concentration is below. Dot-com 2000: 41%. Japan 1989: 44%. Nifty Fifty 1973: ~40%. Today: 40%, and rising. The names rotate. The math doesn't.
Goldman Sachs: 60% of Every S&P Option Traded Is a Call. That Is an All-Time Record.
Goldman's prime brokerage data shows call-buying as a share of total options volume just hit 60%. The previous peaks: 2000, 2007, and early 2021. Retail leverage is at extremes that have historically preceded violent 10–30% pullbacks within 60–90 days.
Section 3 • The Capex Math Is Breaking
$700 Billion of AI Capex on Free Cash Flow That Is Collapsing 28–90% YoY. The Numbers Are Public.
$700B in 2026. Up From $410B Last Year. Up From $50B in 2018.
Bloomberg's spend tracker shows hyperscaler capex more than doubled in 24 months. The historical comparison: 1999 telecom capex peaked at $120B and then fell 70% over the next three years.
Hyperscaler Capex Is Now Outrunning S&P 500 Earnings Growth — by a Multiple.
Free Cash Flow Is Collapsing. The Numbers Are Public.
Hyperscaler FCF Year-over-Year
Microsoft−28%
Alphabet (Google)−89%
Meta−90%
AmazonNegative FCF
The thesis on every AI mega-cap depends on cash flow that is, right now, in the public filings, going the wrong direction. You can spend through one quarter. You can spend through one year. You cannot spend through three on the FCF lines above.
Korea's Export Data Says the Semi Cycle Is at a Peak.
Section 4 • BTC Is Coiled
Every Macro Indicator That Leads BTC Is at All-Time Highs. BTC Has Not Followed Yet. It Has Never Failed to Follow.
M2 vs BTC: The Widest Gap in 5 Years.
US M2 money supply has correlated with BTC at 0.55+ on a 5-year window — popular research cites global M2 + BTC correlation at 0.85–0.90. M2 is now +3.9% YoY and accelerating. BTC has stalled. The recent decoupling is the largest in that window. Historically, BTC closes the gap with a 60–90 day lag.
Gold Leads BTC by ~6 Months. Gold Is at ATHs. BTC Isn't.
Cross-correlation analysis on 2022–2026 daily data finds gold returns lead BTC returns at a best lag of ~230 days. Overlay gold shifted +180 days onto BTC: every major gold breakout has been followed ~6 months later by a BTC breakout. Today: gold at ATH, BTC below its September peak. Pattern implies a BTC target near $121,000, roughly +48% from here.
BTC vs QQQ: Four Divergences in Five Years. Four Resolutions. Same Direction.
QQQ +125% vs BTC +62% since May 2021. The 90-day return correlation collapsed to 0.18 in late 2024 and has rebuilt to 0.48 today — but the price gap hasn't closed. Every prior divergence has resolved with BTC ripping. This is the fifth.
Section 5 • Silver Was HYPE Last Year
Silver Lagged Gold for 2 Years. Then Ran +111% in 8 Weeks. BTC Is Silver in 2026.
From Jan 2023 to mid-2025, gold ripped to ATHs. Silver did nothing. The lag spread peaked at 49 index points in April 2025. Then silver broke. Eight weeks ending late January 2026: SLV +111%, the first time silver broke above $50 since 1980. Silver's full move since Jan 2023: +252% vs gold's +154%. Silver caught up — and overshot.
The analog: BTC has been silver since mid-2025. Gold ripped, BTC stalled. M2 grew, BTC flatlined. AI stocks ran, BTC drifted. The "digital gold" narrative is intact; the price hasn't reflected it. When silver moved, it moved in eight weeks. BTC is closer to that breaking point than at any time since the 2024 ETF launch.

SOXL +996%. MU +756%. AMD 148x Earnings. This Is Not Where You Add — This Is Where You Trim.
12-Month Returns: Who Still Has Room to Run?
P/E vs 5-Year Average: How Stretched Have Multiples Gotten?

SOXL+996%—793x leverage — first to unwind on a tape break

This is not a crash call. It is the observation that when retail accounts have +500% gains sitting in highly cyclical, extreme-multiple names, smart money sells into strength, not out of it. The mega-caps that have already corrected (META −6%, MSFT −9%) are trading near 22x — the market is already rotating away from the obvious overbought names.

The Mechanical Kicker
Every dollar of trading volume on Hyperliquid — whether RD is on the trade or anyone else — generates fees that flow into the buyback engine. 97% buys back and burns HYPE. When AI stocks correct and panic-driven volume floods crypto, that volume itself is a tailwind for HYPE's price, and therefore for every RD share. We win when our trades are right. We also win when the market simply trades.
